title: Market Brief — Tuesday, March 31, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across the board. H100 availability sits at 19% (54/287), H200 at 21% (64/305), and B200 is loosest at 31% (107/350) as new silicon trickles in. The day's headline is H100 spot spiking 27.2% to $1.87/hr, signaling demand surge against thin supply.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across the board. H100 availability sits at 19% (54/287), H200 at 21% (64/305), and B200 is loosest at 31% (107/350) as new silicon trickles in. The day's headline is H100 spot spiking 27.2% to $1.87/hr, signaling demand surge against thin supply.

At TCO-adjusted cost (~$6.83/M tokens on H100), most medium-tier API providers are underwater. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is selling at a loss of roughly $6.45 per million tokens, burning cash to hold share. OpenAI grades Neutral on flagships but Strong on Mini margins; Anthropic Neutral; Google Neutral; DeepSeek Burning Cash; Meta Burning Cash.

B200 at 31% availability and $3.00 floor spot is undervalued compute for inference scaling ahead of tightening. o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued given GPT-5.4 matches capability class at $15/M, sell premium reasoning exposure and rotate into mid-tier.
bullets: 19% of H100 GPUs are available, just 54 of 287 units, near severe shortage territory.
31% B200 availability (107/350) marks the only GPU class above 25%, but watch for tightening.
$1.87 median H100 spot is up 27.2% in a single day.
21.41 on the LLMflation index means API pricing has fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.38 per million output tokens makes DeepSeek V3.2 the cheapest model tracked across all 18.
$80 per million output tokens makes o3 Pro 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2.
